## [00:00 - 03:03]

One of the underrated parts of Facebook and the business manager, I believe, is Creative Hub. So if you haven't seen it, you can click into your mega menu in Business Manager, which is this big menu on the left hand side, or you can visit the link below. But one of the cool things about Creative Hub, when it comes to researching ad ideas and see what other creative ideas other people have come up with, is that Facebook keep this area updated. I believe they review and update it once a month. But what's really cool is as you scroll down, you see so many different examples of what other people are doing, and it just provides so much inspiration. It certainly does for me because I'm looking at what other brands are doing. And a lot of these are big brands. Some of them are not big brands as well. But you can see some ideas of what you can go ahead and apply. Now on the left hand side, you can also then look at different formats. We want to have a look at instant experiences, which are the overlays, the pop-ups that appear in Facebook, and allow you to have a deeper experience as to what the brand is about and what they're doing. So let's find an example here. Let's have a look at Lowe's and see what they're doing. So we click on this ad here, and the video here plays to show you what they're up to. So I don't know if you saw the transition, it starts quite quick, but you land on the ad and you click and then you get like a magazine style interaction. You can go across, you can go down. They've got some video, which is pretty cool. And this gives you an idea of what's possible, because sometimes with Facebook, we can get caught up with what we know and maybe what we see in our newsfeed, but not actually realize how much is possible. So this is a magazine example of something that works for a home improvement brand, and that's great. But you can see so many different ideas. So this is a clothing brand, you can see how they're using it more with video. Let's see what else is going on. So there's a scroll, you can scroll down. And what's cool about this is with an instant experience, you can immerse yourself in Facebook. So you're not leaving Facebook. Often it can work out cheaper, so your CPMs could be better as well. And then you can retarget people based on engagement, but you can also share a lot more of your brand in Facebook without them having to click out. And therefore you can give them a better experience as well, because it's a lot more native. It feels like a mobile app essentially as well. So that's just one example. I don't want to go through into too much detail on instant experiences. But that gives you an idea of what's available in creative home by really do encourage going through it, see what kind of ideas there are, you know, vertical videos is a big one for Instagram stories, you can see a few examples here. You know, this is another underplayed part is people really understanding how to get story ads to work or how to create these kind of more vertical format videos. So have a look through Creative Hub and literally get inspired.
